traits.title: Anxious
traits.description:
## Overview
You seek closeness and reassurance, especially when things feel uncertain. Hypervigilance about distance can drive reaching out more than you intend.

### How it shows
- Frequent check-ins when plans feel vague
- Ruminating about tone or timing
- Relief when contact and plans are steady

### Strengths
- Deep care and emotional attunement
- Motivation to repair and connect

### Growth prompts
- Self-soothe before checking in
- Ask for clear timelines (what/when)
- Track evidence of safety over time

traits.title: Avoidant
traits.description:
## Overview
You value autonomy and calm. Under stress, you may minimize needs or emotionally step back to regain equilibrium.

### How it shows
- Pulling back when conflict rises
- Preferring slow, low-pressure intimacy
- Processing privately before sharing

### Strengths
- Composure and clear boundaries
- Independence and pragmatic thinking

### Growth prompts
- Share needs in small, early ways
- Use time-bound pauses ("back in 30m")
- Practice receiving care without debt

traits.title: Secure
traits.description:
## Overview
You balance closeness and independence. You name needs, assume goodwill, and repair effectively.

### How it shows
- Tolerates ambiguity without spiraling
- Direct, collaborative problem-solving
- Repair that restores connection

### Strengths
- Flexibility and trust-building
- Clear asks and boundaries

### Growth prompts
- Keep modeling clean repair
- Invite mutual check-ins and rituals

pages.description:
# Attachment Style Snapshot
Map your defaults around closeness, conflict, and reassurance.

## What you'll learn
- Your response under uncertainty (seek, step back, collaborate)
- What restores safety fastest for you
- Simple scripts to ask for what you need

## Notes
- There are no right answers, only patterns
- Weigh results by season and context

 This is a reflection tool, not a diagnosis.
When I feel unsure in a close relationship, I usually...

questions.label: seek reassurance or check-in more often
pull back to create space and think
distract myself and wait until it passes

pages.description: When a conflict starts, my first impulse is to...

questions.label: fix it now and seek closeness
downplay it so it doesn't escalate
like relief and safety when it's steady
like pressure; I need breathing room

pages.description: When plans feel uncertain, I tend to...

questions.label: check in for clarity or reassurance
postpone talking until it sorts itself out

pages.description: When I don't get a reply for hours, I...

questions.label: name my need for timeline clarity later
assume best intent until proven otherwise

pages.description: In repair after conflict, I usually...

questions.label: seek closeness quickly to feel safe
own impact and ask for needs
delay repair hoping time fixes it

pages.description: When a partner shares a need that's hard for me, I...

questions.label: negotiate specifics and try small experiments

pages.description: If I fear being too much or not enough, I tend to...

questions.label: seek proof of care (texts, time, words)
have a meta-talk about reassurance patterns
check facts and recent behavior trends

traits.title: Words of Affirmation
traits.description:
## What fills your cup
Specific, sincere words that mirror your effort, character, or needs.

### Signals that land
- Reflective appreciation (what, why it matters)
- Encouraging texts or voice notes
- Clear naming of impact after support

### Tips
- Ask for concrete examples
- Save notes for low days

traits.title: Quality Time
traits.description:
## What fills your cup
Undistracted presence and shared focus.

### Signals that land
- Protected time blocks together
- Devices down, shoulder-to-shoulder activities
- Focused check-ins

### Tips
- Co-create weekly anchor time
- Define "present" norms

traits.title: Acts of Service
traits.description:
## What fills your cup
Helpful actions that reduce friction and add ease.

### Signals that land
- Handling logistics proactively
- Follow-through on promises
- Prep before big days

### Tips
- Share top-3 helpful acts
- Appreciate effort over perfection

traits.title: Physical Touch
traits.description:
## What fills your cup
Warm, consensual touch that co-regulates.

### Signals that land
- Hugs, hand squeezes, cuddles
- Soothing touch during stress
- Playful physicality

### Tips
- Agree on contexts and boundaries
- Pair touch with words when needed

traits.title: Gifts
traits.description:
## What fills your cup
Tangible tokens that symbolize noticing, memory, or care.

### Signals that land
- Small surprises linked to inside jokes
- Useful items that show attentiveness
- Mementos from shared experiences

### Tips
- Keep a "wish list" note
- Balance surprise with practicality

pages.description:
# Love Languages Check-In
Identify the signals of care that land most for you.

## You'll learn
- Your top channels (words, time, acts, touch, gifts)
- Micro-gestures with outsized impact
- Clear ways to ask for care

> You can value more than one; priorities shift by season.
A partner shows love best when they...

questions.label: handle a task that eases my load

pages.description: What feels like care after a hard day?
On an ordinary day, what lifts you most?
Which gesture would you pick on a special day?
During conflict repair, I feel most loved when...
In a long-distance phase, what matters most?
What misfires hurt most when you need care?

questions.label: expensive gifts that miss the mark

traits.title: Turn Toward
traits.description:
## Pattern
You consistently notice and respond to tiny bids for connection.

### Behaviors
- Orient, smile, or ask a follow-up
- Acknowledge even when busy

### Impact
- Trust compounds via micro-moments
- Faster repair under stress

traits.title: Neutral
traits.description:
## Pattern
Sometimes you engage, sometimes you miss bids—often due to distraction.

### Behaviors
- Brief nods without switching tasks
- Mixed consistency across contexts

### Support
- Add "bid windows" in routines
- Use reminders to catch small moments

traits.title: Turn Away
traits.description:
## Pattern
You often miss or deflect bids—particularly under load.

### Behaviors
- Device focus over brief engagement
- Topic changes or jokes to avoid depth

### Repair
- Name misses and invite re-bids
- Practice 10–30s micro-turns

pages.description:
# Bids for Connection Habit
Small moments build big bonds. Measure how often you turn toward.

## Why it matters
- Micro-engagement predicts stability
- Turning toward in stress increases safety

## Use your results
- Set two daily "bid windows"
- Create a re-bid script after misses
When someone shows me a small thing they're excited about...

questions.label: I pause, look, and react positively
I acknowledge briefly but keep doing my thing
I often miss it or say I'm busy
I change topic or redirect to tasks
stay on my device and nod

pages.description: When stressed, my response to bids...

questions.label: is to engage even more intentionally
I joke or deflect instead of engaging
naming and responding to tiny bids
making device rules to avoid turning away

pages.description: When I'm the one bidding, I usually...

questions.label: ask clearly and invite a moment

pages.description: Best habit to raise my turn-toward rate...

questions.label: create a device basket at dinner
label bids out loud ("that was a bid!")

traits.title: Ease with Boundaries
traits.description:
## Pattern
You clearly state limits and renegotiate with care.

### Strengths
- Early, direct communication
- Consistent follow-through

### Growth
- Share brief "why" for buy-in
- Add review dates to agreements

traits.title: Balanced Comfort
traits.description:
## Pattern
Generally comfortable, with a few hot-spots.

### Strengths
- Flexible negotiation
- Context awareness

### Growth
- Script tricky scenarios
- Track resentment as a cue

traits.title: Challenging
traits.description:
## Pattern
Saying no feels risky; over-accommodation is common.

### Signals
- Silent yes followed by resentment
- Indirect or delayed no

### Growth
- Practice tiny, kind no's
- Use "not now, but..." templates

pages.description:
# Boundaries Comfort Index
Gauge how easily you set limits and renegotiate plans.

## Why it matters
- Prevent covert contracts and burnout
- Create mutual clarity and trust

## Use your results
- Adopt 2 scripts for sticky contexts
- Add boundary review rituals
When a plan no longer works for me...

questions.label: I restate needs and propose an alternative
I hint or hope they infer
I avoid and hope it resolves itself
I balance both needs and reschedule
I delay the no and overcommit

pages.description: When someone pushes past my limit...

questions.label: I reassert clearly and hold the line
I renegotiate to find middle ground
I defer and bring it up later
using scripts to say no kindly
checking in with my needs sooner

pages.description: The boundary I most avoid is...

questions.label: I can't do X, I can do Y
My needs changed; I need to rescope

pages.description: One practice to level-up my boundaries...

questions.label: build a script bank for hot-spots
track body cues when I mean no

traits.title: Low Tolerance
traits.description:
## Preference
You prefer clarity, labels, and steady expectations.

### Signals
- Early DTR conversations
- Discomfort with long ambiguity

### Support
- Time-bound clarity checkpoints
- Agreements for exclusivity if desired

traits.title: Medium Tolerance
traits.description:
## Preference
You can handle some ambiguity if care and effort are consistent.

### Signals
- Context-based pacing
- Clarity sought after patterns form

### Support
- Milestone reviews (30/60/90 days)
- Define "what counts" as care

traits.title: High Tolerance
traits.description:
## Preference
Flexible about labels; value flow and evolving definitions.

### Signals
- Comfort with open dynamics
- Trust built through presence

### Support
- Transparent boundaries and calendars
- Explicit risk/need discussions

pages.description:
# Situationship Tolerance Scale
How comfortable are you with ambiguity, labels, and exclusivity?

## You'll learn
- Your comfort range with undefined phases
- What you need to feel steady
- Checkpoints to reduce drift

questions.label: I want to define it soon
I observe a bit, then discuss
I pause dating until it's clearer
open dynamics can work for me
I delay until there's a reason
I feel anxious and seek clarity
I manage it if care is steady
I take space until it settles

pages.description: Markers of progress for me are...

questions.label: more regular time on each other's calendar
greater social visibility of the relationship

pages.description: If we disagree on pace, I...

questions.label: continue with openness and no labels

pages.description: A helpful agreement template would be...

traits.title: Low Jealousy
traits.description:
## Baseline
Jealousy is rare; trust and self-regulation are strong.

### Strengths
- Calm appraisal of context
- Internal soothing skills

### Tips
- Share internal process to support partners
- Keep agreements visible and simple

traits.title: Context-Sensitive
traits.description:
## Baseline
Certain contexts trigger you; clarity and communication help.

### Triggers
- Secrecy, inconsistent signals
- Past-pattern echoes

### Tips
- Pre-plan scripts for hot contexts
- Define "green/amber/red" zones together

traits.title: High Jealousy
traits.description:
## Baseline
Jealous feelings arise often; reassurance and explicit agreements help.

### Patterns
- Comparison loops, hypervigilance
- Fast reassurance seeking

### Tips
- Time-bound reassurance + tracking safety evidence
- Design shared rituals that replenish security

pages.description:
# Jealousy Triggers Map
Find contexts that trigger jealousy and the responses that help.

## You'll learn
- Your main triggers and coping patterns
- Agreements that reduce spikes
- Rituals that restore safety
Seeing my partner engage closely with others...

questions.label: social media cues or hidden messages

pages.description: Social context that spikes me is...

questions.label: repair after small ruptures is clean

traits.title: Rapid Responder
traits.description:
## Baseline
Fast replies feel natural; immediacy over depth.

### Strengths
- Momentum and clarity
- Low friction for coordination

### Growth
- Add context when brief
- Use "later reply" signals when busy

traits.title: Thoughtful Texter
traits.description:
## Baseline
You prefer considered replies; depth over speed.

### Strengths
- Clarity and nuance
- Lower misreads

### Growth
- Set expectations for reply windows
- Use quick "seen + later" when needed

traits.title: Laid-Back Messenger
traits.description:
## Baseline
You text in waves; casual norms and flexible timing.

### Strengths
- Easygoing coordination
- Low pressure in threads

### Growth
- Add structure for time-sensitive topics
- Label urgency/priority when needed

pages.description:
# Texting Style Decoder
Understand your reply speed, tone, norms, and expectations.

## Use this
- Set shared texting norms
- Prevent misreads and pressure cycles
If we mismatch, best fix is...

traits.title: Green-Flag Focus
traits.description:
## Orientation
You notice and prioritize healthy patterns and strengths.

### Markers
- Repair after conflict
- Accountability and kindness

### Tips
- Keep celebrating prosocial signals
- Name what works to reinforce it

traits.title: Beige-Balanced
traits.description:
## Orientation
You weigh context and neutrality before judging.

### Markers
- Logistics care, consistency
- Curiosity before conclusions

### Tips
- Use "needs + request" when ambiguous
- Track patterns over snapshots

traits.title: Red-Flag Sensitive
traits.description:
## Orientation
You quickly spot risk patterns and act to protect yourself.

### Markers
- Coercion, contempt, secrecy
- Ghosting, benching patterns

### Tips
- Trust early signals
- Exit cleanly when needed

pages.description:
# Green/Beige/Red Flags Filter
Define what you rate as green, beige, and red—for you.

## Why it helps
- Faster pattern recognition
- Clearer boundaries and asks
Early in dating, I mostly look for...

questions.label: I name it and see response
I note it, gather more data

pages.description: An amber (watch) flag for me is...
Green signals I want to prioritize...
Response to a red flag I choose is...

questions.label: make a specific request and timeline
I balance and wait for patterns
I'm sensitive to red and act fast

traits.title: Leader
traits.description:
## Orientation
You naturally take the lead, self-advocate, and drive momentum.

### Strengths
- Initiative and clarity
- Comfort with visibility

### Growth
- Invite collaboration and rotate roles
- Balance drive with recovery

traits.title: Balanced
traits.description:
## Orientation
You switch between leading and supporting as needed.

### Strengths
- Flexibility and situational awareness
- Team-mindedness

### Growth
- Name role expectations early
- Avoid over-functioning in gaps

traits.title: Supportive
traits.description:
## Orientation
You enable others to shine and prefer behind-the-scenes.

### Strengths
- Stabilizing presence
- Detail care

### Growth
- Practice visible asks and wins
- Take low-stakes spotlight reps

pages.description:
# Main Character Energy Meter
How strongly you lead, self-advocate, and enjoy visibility.

traits.title: Type A
traits.description:
## Orientation
High pace, structure, and outcome focus.

### Strengths
- Drive and planning
- Clear ownership

### Growth
- Add slack and buffers
- Protect recovery time

traits.title: Balanced
traits.description:
## Orientation
Mix of planning and ease depending on stakes.

### Strengths
- Flexible execution
- Context-driven pace

### Growth
- Make decision rules explicit
- Avoid drift via checkpoints

traits.title: Type B
traits.description:
## Orientation
Easygoing pace, adaptive flow, low pressure by default.

### Strengths
- Calm and creativity
- Stress resilience

### Growth
- Add light structure for deadlines
- Use timeboxing to start

pages.description:
# Type A / Type B Balance
Your pace, control, and pressure preferences in daily life.

questions.label: loose plan, adapt as I go

traits.title: Introvert
traits.description:
## Baseline
Recharge solo or in low-stim settings; depth over breadth.

### Strengths
- Focus and deep connection
- Thoughtful presence

### Tips
- Protect recharge windows
- Choose depth-first plans

traits.title: Ambivert
traits.description:
## Baseline
Enjoy both solo and social time; balance is key.

### Strengths
- Flexibility across contexts
- Adaptive energy

### Tips
- Calendar anchor + free day
- Mix group and 1:1 time

traits.title: Extrovert
traits.description:
## Baseline
Gain energy from groups and active environments.

### Strengths
- Momentum and network building
- Enthusiasm and warmth

### Tips
- Add recovery sprints
- Keep an eye on over-scheduling

pages.description:
# Social Battery Gauge
How you recharge and what drains you fastest.

traits.title: Low
traits.description:
## Baseline
You rarely trade your needs for harmony; friction is tolerated.

### Strengths
- Directness and clarity
- Self-respect and boundaries

### Tips
- Keep kindness + firmness paired
- Invite others' needs explicitly

traits.title: Moderate
traits.description:
## Baseline
You sometimes over-give or delay no's. Awareness helps recalibrate.

### Signals
- Subtle resentment after yes
- Delayed boundary setting

### Tips
- Use tiny, early no's
- Track "should" spikes

traits.title: High
traits.description:
## Baseline
You often sideline needs to avoid conflict or rejection.

### Signals
- Chronic over-commitment
- Anxiety around others' feelings

### Tips
- Script gentle no's in advance
- Practice tolerating short-term discomfort

pages.description:
# People-Pleasing Tendencies (Fawn)
Where you trade needs for harmony and how often.
When asked for a favor I can't do...

questions.label: I say maybe and feel torn
I say yes and resent later
I delay and hope it passes
body says no but mouth says yes
'I can't do X; I can do Y'

traits.title: Spontaneous
traits.description:
## Orientation
You thrive with freedom and last-minute flow.

### Strengths
- Creativity and responsiveness
- Enjoy discovery

### Tips
- Add one anchor to reduce friction
- Keep backup options lightweight

traits.title: Balanced
traits.description:
## Orientation
You like light structure with room for surprises.

### Strengths
- Adaptable planning
- Good trade-off sense

### Tips
- Protect anchors in the calendar
- Leave intentional white space

traits.title: Planner
traits.description:
## Orientation
You prefer structure and advance planning for ease.

### Strengths
- Predictability and preparedness
- Lower decision fatigue

### Tips
- Build flex buffers
- Try small spontaneous reps

pages.description:
# Spontaneity vs Planning Dial
Find your sweet spot between last-minute flow and structure.
For a weekend plan I prefer...

questions.label: fine if I keep one anchor

traits.title: Low
traits.description:
## Baseline
Prefer safety, proven paths, and measured change.

### Strengths
- Stability and endurance
- Downside protection

### Tips
- Take tiny experimental reps
- Use capped-risk trials

traits.title: Moderate
traits.description:
## Baseline
Calculated risks with safeguards and reviews.

### Strengths
- Balanced portfolios
- Sensible timing

### Tips
- Define stop-loss and success metrics
- Review cadence (monthly/quarterly)

traits.title: High
traits.description:
## Baseline
Comfortable with bold bets for outsized upside.

### Strengths
- Momentum and innovation
- Tolerance for ambiguity

### Tips
- Add risk caps and contingencies
- Track downside explicitly

pages.description:
# Risk Appetite Check
How you approach risk across money, career, relationships, and health.

This test helps you look into your emotional and physical state over the past two weeks. 
 # It shows:
• how much you feel cheerful and calm in your everyday life,
• whether you have enough energy for things that matter to you,
• how filled your life is with things that inspire you,
• which areas currently need more attention and support.

In the end, the test gives you a chance to see your current level of inner resources and understand what specifically might help restore balance. It's not about diagnosis or judgment — it's about awareness of your state, so you can care for yourself more consciously.

Over the past 2 weeks, I...
felt cheerful and in good spirits

questions.label: More than half of the time
Less than half of the time

pages.description:
Over the past 2 weeks, I...
felt calm and relaxed

questions.label: More than half of the time
Less than half of the time

pages.description:
Over the past 2 weeks, I...
felt active and full of energy

questions.label: More than half of the time
Less than half of the time

pages.description:
Over the past 2 weeks, I...
woke up feeling fresh and rested

questions.label: More than half of the time
Less than half of the time

pages.description:
Over the past 2 weeks, I...
had daily life filled with interesting things

questions.label: More than half of the time
Less than half of the time

excellent.title: Excellent Level
excellent.intro: Your results indicate a high level of psychological well-being. You are in a state of inner harmony and life satisfaction.

good.title: Good Level
good.intro: Overall, you are feeling well, but there is room for improvement. Your well-being is stable, however some aspects require attention.

moderate.title: Moderate Level
moderate.intro: Your results suggest there is room for growth in your well-being There are clear signals that it's worth paying attention to your mental and physical health.

low.title: Lower Than Usual Level
low.intro: The results indicate that things are not easy for you right now. If this continues for more than 2 weeks and affects your daily life, it may be helpful to talk about your condition with a psychologist or psychotherapist.

critical.title: Very Low Level
critical.intro: Your answers indicate noticeable difficulties. Support from a qualified specialist can help you restore your resources and find an effective action plan.

emotional.high: Your emotional state is stable and positive. You manage your mood well and maintain inner peace.
emotional.medium: Emotional balance is periodically disrupted. Mood fluctuates, and it's not always possible to maintain calm in everyday situations.
emotional.low: Maintaining a positive mood and inner peace is difficult right now. The emotional sphere may require additional attention and support.
tips: Practice daily gratitude: write down 3 positive moments of the day
Try breathing techniques for quick calming (stop if you feel dizzy)
Try to set a daily social media limit (for example, 20-30 minutes)
Talk to a close person about your feelings

physical.high: Your energy levels and sleep quality are high. Your physical resources allow you to be productive and active.
physical.medium: Your energy levels and sleep quality fluctuate periodically. This may indicate disruptions in routine or overload.
physical.low: Your answers point to fatigue and possible sleep difficulties that affect quality of life. Physical resources need recovery
tips: Go to bed and wake up at about the same time each day
void caffeine after 2:00 PM and gadgets an hour before bed
Add 20–30 minutes of daily physical activity (if there are no contraindications)
Consider consulting a doctor if fatigue persists
Create a bedtime ritual (warm shower, reading, meditation)

engagement.high: Your life is filled with meaning and engaging activities. You feel involved and motivated in everyday matters.
engagement.medium: Interest in daily activities varies: periods of enthusiasm alternate with decreased motivation. You are searching for new sources of inspiration.
engagement.low: It’s difficult to find satisfaction in things that used to bring joy. Life feels less rich and interesting.
tips: Set aside 1 hour per week for a new hobby or skill
Plan something pleasant every day, even if it's small things
Return to activities that once brought joy
Find a community of like-minded people (online or offline)

warnings.sleep: 💤 Your answers suggest possible sleep difficulties. If this persists or worsens, consulting a doctor can help identify causes and solutions.
warnings.noInterest: 💭 Decreased interest in daily activities may be a reaction to stress or indicate a need for support. If this feeling doesn't pass, a psychologist or psychotherapist can help.
warnings.needSupport: 🚨 If there is an immediate risk to yourself or others, contact local emergency services right away. 🤝 If you need urgent emotional support, reach out to a crisis hotline in your country or to the nearest emergency service.
